title: [$250] Receipt selected from gallery can upload as empty on iOS mobile web

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Problem On iOS mobile web (Chrome or Safari on iPhone), if a user selects a receipt photo from their gallery and briefly switches apps before submitting, the file can silently lose its content. The receipt appears to upload but arrives at the server as 0 bytes, showing the user "There was a problem creating the receipt." ## Solution Read the selected file into memory immediately after it's picked, before the user has a chance to background the app. iOS can revoke access to gallery-picked files after a short time, but files held in memory aren't affected.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @linhvovan29546

title: [$250] Perf: ReportWelcomeText maps all personal details on every render

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Problem `ReportWelcomeText` calls `useMappedPersonalDetails(personalDetailMapper)`, which walks the entire `PERSONAL_DETAILS_LIST` and produces a projected copy of every personal detail on every render. The component only consumes personal details for a handful of participants (via `getPersonalDetailsForAccountIDs(participantAccountIDsExcludeCurrentUser, personalDetails)`), which indexes by account ID — so the full mapping is wasted work. On an account with ~11k personal details (iOS), render time of `ReportWelcomeText` varies from 50 to 500ms (depending on a report), which is a noticeable source of jank when opening reports. ## Solution Swap the mapped hook for raw `useOnyx(ONYXKEYS.PERSONAL_DETAILS_LIST)`. Re-render surface is unchanged (Onyx still uses shallowEqual on the raw collection), but per-render cost drops from ~270ms to a few key lookups (stable ~2ms). ## Fix Tracked by [#88511](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/88511).
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @TMisiukiewicz

**Bug source:** Exploratory - Significant User Experience Deterioration
**Device used:** iPhone 15 iOS 26,3,1
**App Component:** Money Requests

## Action Performed:
1. Sign in to ND with a new account
2. Create a 1:1 chat with another user
3. Create an expense in the chat
4. Open the expense and tap the parent chat link in the header
5. Tap the expense again, tap “More options,” and then tap “Delete.”
6. Tap Back button

## Expected Result:
User is able to navigate away from the chat after deleting an expense

## Actual Result:
User is stuck in the DM chat. On Prod the app crashes

## Workaround:
Unknown

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.61-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87739 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** testing210790@gmail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Pull Request QA execution **Device used:** Windows 11 **App Component:** Other ## Action Performed: 1. Open the app 2. Complete onboarding as workspace admin 3. Select an accounting integration (e.g. QuickBooks Online) 4. Enter invalid credentials to force connection failure 5. Navigate to Home page ## Expected Result: The "Getting Started" section is displayed on the Home page and includes the "Connect to [integration]" task, which remains visible and unchecked after a failed connection attempt. ## Actual Result: The "Getting Started" section is not displayed on the Home page after the failed accounting integration. As a result, the "Connect to [integration]" task is missing and the user is not prompted to complete the setup. The issue was reproduced in Chile and also tested using a USA location (Miami) via VPN. In both cases, the "Getting Started" section was not displayed after onboarding and failed accounting connection. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/75918d93-aad9-412d-821b-5dda887c742a [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

On the compose box, click "+" icon and choose "assign task" 2. Type any title then click "next" and click "confirm task" 3. Open the task that just created 4. Type "Ship it @someone@domain.com" and send it 5. Wait for several mins 6. Try with typing "Ship it to @someone@domain.com" and send it 7. Wait for several mins ## Expected Result: After typing "Ship it @someone@domain.com", the task should be assigned to that user (Assignee field filled with the user mentioned) ## Actual Result: ype "ship to @someone@domain.com" doesn't assigned to the user mentioned ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/266cfe95-a1e6-4e34-a113-b87a45798352 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.61-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/folder/13176668/test-cases/93408141 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** - **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** Ventura / Chrome **App Component:** Other ## Action Performed: Preconditions: use a VPN if not located in US. Cookies setup required for A/B tests: 1. Open an incognito window 2. Navigate to new.expensify.com (NewDot) (Very important) 3. Open console > Application > Cookies 4. Set the following cookies (check video for instructions) lc-qa = 1, lc-debug=1 5. Refresh the page Note: To bypass these conditions during QA, set the lc-qa-mocked=1 cookie. This will ignore country and free trial checks STR: 1. Sign in with an account with expired free trial and control workspace 2. Navigate to Workspace settings > add a member (Should have access to the account) 3, Navigate to Reports > Insights ## Expected Result: The banner appears on "Spend over time", "Top categories", "Top merchants", and "Top spenders" pages: "Earn up to 2% cash back on all US purchases" and CTA: "Get started" and "X" to close the banner ## Actual Result: No banner appears ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a7483ca3-8b4f-405c-9931-da5ccb44ba34 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
